5. Look at how providers expose image models - search for patterns like `image(modelId)` or `imageModel`.
6. Read the complete implementation of at least one image model provider class.

I want to understand the full interface chain from `@ai-sdk/provider` specs → `@ai-sdk/provider-utils` helpers → concrete provider image model implementation.

input.prompt:
I need to understand the factory function pattern for AI SDK providers and the package configuration. Please explore:

1. Read a simple provider's `src/index.ts` to understand the `createXxx` factory function pattern and the provider object shape.
2. Look at a provider's `package.json` to understand the required fields, exports map, build config.
3. Read `tsconfig.json` in a provider package.
4. Look at any `tsup.config.ts` or build configuration in provider packages.
5. Look at the root `tsconfig.json` to understand how packages register themselves.
6. Search for any `createProvider` or `createXxx` patterns to understand the factory interface - specifically look for `ProviderV1` or `ImageModelProviderV1` interfaces in `@ai-sdk/provider`.
7. Look for how providers that ONLY do images (no text/chat) are structured - check if there's a minimal provider interface.

Focus on: `packages/fal/`, `packages/luma/`, or `packages/stability` if they exist, otherwise `packages/openai/src/openai-provider.ts` and similar files.

1	# Add new provider
2	
3	## `@ai-sdk/<provider>` vs 3rd party package
4	
5	Every provider is welcome to create a 3rd party package. We are happy to link to it from our documentation.
6	
7	If you would prefer a 1st party `@ai-sdk/<provider>` package, please create an issue first to discuss.
8	
9	## Example
10	
11	https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/8136/files
12	
13	## How
14	
15	1. Create new folder `packages/<provider>`
16	2. Set version in `packages/<provider>/package.json` to `0.0.0`
17	3. Create changeset for new package with `major`
18	4. Add examples to `examples/ai-functions/src/*/<provider>.ts` depending on what model types the provider supports
19	5. Add documentation in `content/providers/01-ai-sdk-providers/<last number + 10>-<provider>.mdx`
20	
21	See also [providers.md](providers.md)
22	
23	## When in pre-release mode
24	
25	If `main` is set up to publish `beta` releases, no further action is necessary. Just make sure not to backport it to the `vX.Y` stable branch since it will result in an npm version conflict once we exit pre-release mode on `main`
26

1	# AI SDK - fal Provider
2	
3	The **[fal provider](https://ai-sdk.dev/providers/ai-sdk-providers/fal)** for the [AI SDK](https://ai-sdk.dev/docs) contains image model support for the [fal.ai API](https://fal.ai/).
4	
5	## Setup
6	
7	The fal provider is available in the `@ai-sdk/fal` module. You can install it with
8	
9	```bash
10	npm i @ai-sdk/fal
11	```
12	
13	## Skill for Coding Agents
14	
15	If you use coding agents such as Claude Code or Cursor, we highly recommend adding the AI SDK skill to your repository:
16	
17	```shell
18	npx skills add vercel/ai
19	```
20	
21	## Provider Instance
22	
23	You can import the default provider instance `fal` from `@ai-sdk/fal`:
24	
25	```ts
26	import { fal } from '@ai-sdk/fal';
27	```
28	
29	## Image Generation Example
30	
31	```ts
32	import { fal } from '@ai-sdk/fal';
33	import { generateImage } from 'ai';
34	import fs from 'fs';
35	const { image } = await generateImage({
36	  model: fal.image('fal-ai/flux/schnell'),
37	  prompt: 'A cat wearing a intricate robe',
38	});
39	
40	const filename = `image-${Date.now()}.png`;
41	fs.writeFileSync(filename, image.uint8Array);
42	console.log(`Image saved to ${filename}`);
43	```
44	
45	## Additional Options
46	
47	If you want to pass additional inputs to the model besides the prompt, use the `providerOptions.fal` property:
48	
49	```ts
50	const { image } = await generateImage({
51	  model: fal.image('fal-ai/recraft-v3'),
52	  prompt: 'A cat wearing a intricate robe',
53	  size: '1920x1080',
54	  providerOptions: {
55	    fal: {
56	      style: 'digital_illustration',
57	    },
58	  },
59	});
60	```
61	
62	## Documentation
63	
64	Please check out the **[fal provider](https://ai-sdk.dev/providers/ai-sdk-providers/fal)** for more information.
65

## Summary: Vercel AI SDK Provider Package Architecture

I've thoroughly explored the Vercel AI SDK monorepo and analyzed two key image-capable provider packages: **fal** and **togetherai**. Here's a complete breakdown of the provider package structure and architecture patterns.

### 1. PROVIDER PACKAGES OVERVIEW

The monorepo contains 60+ packages under `/packages/`. Key provider packages include:
- Image generation: **fal**, **togetherai**, **replicate**, **black-forest-labs**, **deepinfra**, etc.
- Language models: **openai**, **anthropic**, **google**, **mistral**, **groq**, **cohere**, etc.
- Specialized: **assemblyai** (transcription), **elevenlabs** (speech), **deepgram** (speech-to-text)
- Infrastructure: **provider** (base specs), **provider-utils** (shared utilities)

### 2. COMPLETE FILE STRUCTURE FOR PROVIDER PACKAGES

#### Standard Directory Layout:
```
packages/<provider>/
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts                    # Main export file
│   ├── <provider>-provider.ts      # Provider factory & interface
│   ├── <provider>-image-model.ts   # Image model implementation
│   ├── <provider>-image-settings.ts # Model IDs (string literals)
│   ├── <provider>-image-options.ts  # Provider options schemas
│   ├── <provider>-chat-options.ts   # Chat model options (if supported)
│   ├── version.ts                   # Version string export
│   ├── <model>.test.ts              # Unit tests
│   ├── <model>-test.mp3             # Test fixtures
│   └── __fixtures__/                # JSON test fixtures
├── package.json                     # Package metadata
├── tsconfig.json                    # TypeScript config (extends shared)
├── tsconfig.build.json              # Build-specific config
├── tsup.config.ts                   # Build configuration
├── vitest.node.config.js            # Node environment tests
├── vitest.edge.config.js            # Edge environment tests
├── turbo.json                        # Monorepo task config
├── README.md                         # Provider documentation
└── CHANGELOG.md                      # Version history
```

### 3. PACKAGE.JSON PATTERNS

Both fal and togetherai follow identical patterns:

```json
{
  "name": "@ai-sdk/<provider>",
  "version": "2.0.x",
  "license": "Apache-2.0",
  "sideEffects": false,
  "main": "./dist/index.js",
  "module": "./dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    "./package.json": "./package.json",
    ".": {
      "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
      "import": "./dist/index.mjs",
      "require": "./dist/index.js"
    }
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@ai-sdk/provider": "workspace:*",
    "@ai-sdk/provider-utils": "workspace:*"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@ai-sdk/test-server": "workspace:*",
    "@types/node": "20.17.24",
    "@vercel/ai-tsconfig": "workspace:*",
    "tsup": "^8",
    "typescript": "5.8.3",
    "zod": "3.25.76"
  },
  "peerDependencies": {
    "zod": "^3.25.76 || ^4.1.8"
  }
}
```

**Note:** togetherai has additional dependency: `@ai-sdk/openai-compatible` (for leveraging OpenAI-compatible APIs)

### 4. BUILD CONFIGURATION

**tsup.config.ts** (identical for both providers):
```typescript
// Builds both CommonJS and ESM, generates .d.ts files, sourcemaps
// Injects package version at build time via __PACKAGE_VERSION__
```

**tsconfig.json**:
- Extends shared config: `@vercel/ai-tsconfig/ts-library.json`
- Has composite: true for workspace references
- References dependencies: `provider`, `provider-utils`, optionally `openai-compatible`

**tsconfig.build.json**:
- Extends tsconfig.json
- Disables composite for tsup builds

### 5. CORE EXPORT PATTERNS (src/index.ts)

**FAL Provider:**
```typescript
export type { FalProvider, FalProviderSettings };
export { createFal, fal };
export type { FalImageModelOptions };
export type { FalSpeechModelOptions };
export type { FalTranscriptionModelOptions };
export type { FalVideoModelOptions };
export type { FalVideoModelId };
export { VERSION };
```

**TogetherAI Provider:**
```typescript
export type { TogetherAIProvider, TogetherAIProviderSettings };
export { createTogetherAI, togetherai };
export type { TogetherAIImageModelOptions };
export type { TogetherAIRerankingModelOptions };
export { VERSION };
```

### 6. PROVIDER FACTORY PATTERN

**Standard Interface (from provider.ts):**
```typescript
export interface FalProvider extends ProviderV3 {
  image(modelId: FalImageModelId): ImageModelV3;
  imageModel(modelId: FalImageModelId): ImageModelV3;
  transcription(modelId: FalTranscriptionModelId): TranscriptionModelV3;
  video(modelId: FalVideoModelId): Experimental_VideoModelV3;
  speech(modelId: FalSpeechModelId): SpeechModelV3;
  embeddingModel(modelId: string): never; // Throws NoSuchModelError
}

export function createFal(options: FalProviderSettings = {}): FalProvider {
  const baseURL = withoutTrailingSlash(options.baseURL ?? defaultBaseURL);
  const getHeaders = () => 
    withUserAgentSuffix({ Authorization: `Key ${apiKey}` }, 
                         `ai-sdk/fal/${VERSION}`);
  
  return {
    specificationVersion: 'v3',
    image: (modelId) => new FalImageModel(modelId, { ... }),
    imageModel: (modelId) => new FalImageModel(modelId, { ... }),
    // ... other model methods
  };
}

export const fal = createFal();
```

### 7. MODEL IMPLEMENTATION PATTERNS

**Image Model Classes (implements ImageModelV3):**
```typescript
export class FalImageModel implements ImageModelV3 {
  readonly specificationVersion = 'v3';
  readonly maxImagesPerCall = 1;
  
  constructor(
    readonly modelId: FalImageModelId,
    private readonly config: FalImageModelConfig
  ) {}
  
  async doGenerate(
    options: Parameters<ImageModelV3['doGenerate']>[0]
  ): Promise<Awaited<ReturnType<ImageModelV3['doGenerate']>>> {
    // Parse provider options with Zod schema
    // Convert files to data URIs
    // Make API request via postJsonToApi helper
    // Download generated images
    // Return result with warnings and metadata
  }
}
```

### 8. OPTIONS & SETTINGS PATTERNS

**Model ID Types (fal-image-settings.ts):**
```typescript
export type FalImageModelId = 
  | 'fal-ai/flux/schnell'
  | 'fal-ai/flux/dev'
  | 'fal-ai/flux-pro/v1.1'
  | (string & {}); // Allow custom model IDs

export type FalImageSize =
  | 'square' | 'square_hd'
  | 'landscape_16_9' | 'landscape_4_3'
  | 'portrait_16_9' | 'portrait_4_3'
  | { width: number; height: number };
```

**Provider Options Schema (fal-image-options.ts):**
```typescript
// Uses Zod for runtime validation and TypeScript type inference
export const falImageModelOptionsSchema = lazySchema(() =>
  zodSchema(
    z.object({
      guidanceScale: z.number().min(1).max(20).nullish(),
      numInferenceSteps: z.number().min(1).max(50).nullish(),
      enableSafetyChecker: z.boolean().nullish(),
      outputFormat: z.enum(['jpeg', 'png']).nullish(),
      // ... handles camelCase and deprecated snake_case versions
      // Includes .passthrough() to allow unknown props
    })
  ),
);
```

### 9. SHARED DEPENDENCIES

**@ai-sdk/provider:**
- Defines model interfaces: `ImageModelV3`, `LanguageModelV3`, `SpeechModelV3`, etc.
- Defines `ProviderV3` interface for factory functions
- Provides error types like `NoSuchModelError`

**@ai-sdk/provider-utils:**
- HTTP utilities: `postJsonToApi`, `getFromApi`, `combineHeaders`
- Response handlers: `createJsonResponseHandler`, `createJsonErrorResponseHandler`
- Schema utilities: `zodSchema`, `lazySchema`, `InferSchema`, `parseProviderOptions`
- Data conversion: `convertImageModelFileToDataUri`
- API key loading: `loadApiKey`, `withUserAgentSuffix`, `withoutTrailingSlash`

### 10. TEST PATTERNS

**Test Files:**
- `*.test.ts` files alongside source code
- Use Vitest with separate configs for Node and Edge environments
- Include snapshot tests (`.snap` files)
- Test fixtures in `__fixtures__/` directory
- Package version injected at test time

**Scripts in package.json:**
```json
{
  "test": "pnpm test:node && pnpm test:edge",
  "test:node": "vitest --config vitest.node.config.js --run",
  "test:edge": "vitest --config vitest.edge.config.js --run",
  "type-check": "tsc --build"
}
```

### 11. VERSION MANAGEMENT

**version.ts** (identical pattern):
```typescript
declare const __PACKAGE_VERSION__: string | undefined;
export const VERSION: string =
  typeof __PACKAGE_VERSION__ !== 'undefined'
    ? __PACKAGE_VERSION__
    : '0.0.0-test';
```

- Version injected at build time via tsup config
- Used in User-Agent headers for API calls

### 12. CONTRIBUTING DOCUMENTATION

**add-new-provider.md workflow:**
1. Create new folder `packages/<provider>`
2. Set version to `0.0.0` in package.json
3. Create changeset for new package with `major`
4. Add examples to `examples/ai-functions/src/*/<provider>.ts`
5. Add documentation to `content/providers/01-ai-sdk-providers/`

**provider-architecture.md:**
- Shows layered architecture with adapter pattern
- Core: `ai` package
- Specifications: `@ai-sdk/provider` 
- Utilities: `@ai-sdk/provider-utils`
- Implementations: Provider packages (e.g., `@ai-sdk/fal`)

### 13. KEY DIFFERENCES BETWEEN PROVIDERS

**FAL:**
- Supports: Image, Video, Speech, Transcription
- Custom image handling with multiple input support
- Downloads generated images from URLs
- Custom error handling for validation errors
- Handles both camelCase and deprecated snake_case options

**TogetherAI:**
- Supports: Chat, Completion, Embedding, Image, Reranking
- Leverages `@ai-sdk/openai-compatible` for chat/completion/embedding
- Custom image implementation
- Simpler API contract due to OpenAI compatibility layer
- Base64-encoded image responses

### 14. CRITICAL IMPLEMENTATION INSIGHTS

1. **All providers implement specification v3**: `specificationVersion: 'v3'`
2. **Use ProviderV3 factory pattern**: One function creates the provider with methods for each model type
3. **Zod schemas required**: All provider options use Zod with `.passthrough()` for extensibility
4. **Lazy schema evaluation**: Use `lazySchema()` to defer schema creation
5. **User-Agent suffixes**: Track SDK usage via headers
6. **Workspace dependencies**: Use `workspace:*` protocol for monorepo package references
7. **Error handling**: Create custom response handlers that parse provider-specific error formats
8. **API key management**: Load from environment variables with fallbacks
9. **File paths**: Use absolute paths in all documentation (following monorepo pattern)

All files are located in:

- **togetherai**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775479294297-s6dako/packages/togetherai/`
- **Contributing docs**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775479294297-s6dako/contributing/`

### 8. OPTIONS & SETTINGS PATTERNS

**Model ID Types (fal-image-settings.ts):**
```typescript
export type FalImageModelId = 
  | 'fal-ai/flux/schnell'
  | 'fal-ai/flux/dev'
  | 'fal-ai/flux-pro/v1.1'
  | (string & {}); // Allow custom model IDs

export type FalImageSize =
  | 'square' | 'square_hd'
  | 'landscape_16_9' | 'landscape_4_3'
  | 'portrait_16_9' | 'portrait_4_3'
  | { width: number; height: number };
```

**Provider Options Schema (fal-image-options.ts):**
```typescript
// Uses Zod for runtime validation and TypeScript type inference
export const falImageModelOptionsSchema = lazySchema(() =>
  zodSchema(
    z.object({
      guidanceScale: z.number().min(1).max(20).nullish(),
      numInferenceSteps: z.number().min(1).max(50).nullish(),
      enableSafetyChecker: z.boolean().nullish(),
      outputFormat: z.enum(['jpeg', 'png']).nullish(),
      // ... handles camelCase and deprecated snake_case versions
      // Includes .passthrough() to allow unknown props
    })
  ),
);
```

### 9. SHARED DEPENDENCIES

**@ai-sdk/provider:**
- Defines model interfaces: `ImageModelV3`, `LanguageModelV3`, `SpeechModelV3`, etc.
- Defines `ProviderV3` interface for factory functions
- Provides error types like `NoSuchModelError`

**@ai-sdk/provider-utils:**
- HTTP utilities: `postJsonToApi`, `getFromApi`, `combineHeaders`
- Response handlers: `createJsonResponseHandler`, `createJsonErrorResponseHandler`
- Schema utilities: `zodSchema`, `lazySchema`, `InferSchema`, `parseProviderOptions`
- Data conversion: `convertImageModelFileToDataUri`
- API key loading: `loadApiKey`, `withUserAgentSuffix`, `withoutTrailingSlash`

### 10. TEST PATTERNS

**Test Files:**
- `*.test.ts` files alongside source code
- Use Vitest with separate configs for Node and Edge environments
- Include snapshot tests (`.snap` files)
- Test fixtures in `__fixtures__/` directory
- Package version injected at test time

**Scripts in package.json:**
```json
{
  "test": "pnpm test:node && pnpm test:edge",
  "test:node": "vitest --config vitest.node.config.js --run",
  "test:edge": "vitest --config vitest.edge.config.js --run",
  "type-check": "tsc --build"
}
```

### 11. VERSION MANAGEMENT

**version.ts** (identical pattern):
```typescript
declare const __PACKAGE_VERSION__: string | undefined;
export const VERSION: string =
  typeof __PACKAGE_VERSION__ !== 'undefined'
    ? __PACKAGE_VERSION__
    : '0.0.0-test';
```

- Version injected at build time via tsup config
- Used in User-Agent headers for API calls

### 12. CONTRIBUTING DOCUMENTATION

**add-new-provider.md workflow:**
1. Create new folder `packages/<provider>`
2. Set version to `0.0.0` in package.json
3. Create changeset for new package with `major`
4. Add examples to `examples/ai-functions/src/*/<provider>.ts`
5. Add documentation to `content/providers/01-ai-sdk-providers/`

**provider-architecture.md:**
- Shows layered architecture with adapter pattern
- Core: `ai` package
- Specifications: `@ai-sdk/provider` 
- Utilities: `@ai-sdk/provider-utils`
- Implementations: Provider packages (e.g., `@ai-sdk/fal`)

### 13. KEY DIFFERENCES BETWEEN PROVIDERS

**FAL:**
- Supports: Image, Video, Speech, Transcription
- Custom image handling with multiple input support
- Downloads generated images from URLs
- Custom error handling for validation errors
- Handles both camelCase and deprecated snake_case options

**TogetherAI:**
- Supports: Chat, Completion, Embedding, Image, Reranking
- Leverages `@ai-sdk/openai-compatible` for chat/completion/embedding
- Custom image implementation
- Simpler API contract due to OpenAI compatibility layer
- Base64-encoded image responses

### 14. CRITICAL IMPLEMENTATION INSIGHTS

1. **All providers implement specification v3**: `specificationVersion: 'v3'`
2. **Use ProviderV3 factory pattern**: One function creates the provider with methods for each model type
3. **Zod schemas required**: All provider options use Zod with `.passthrough()` for extensibility
4. **Lazy schema evaluation**: Use `lazySchema()` to defer schema creation
5. **User-Agent suffixes**: Track SDK usage via headers
6. **Workspace dependencies**: Use `workspace:*` protocol for monorepo package references
7. **Error handling**: Create custom response handlers that parse provider-specific error formats
8. **API key management**: Load from environment variables with fallbacks
9. **File paths**: Use absolute paths in all documentation (following monorepo pattern)

All files are located in:

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the image generation implementation. Let me create a detailed report.

## Summary Report: Image Generation in Vercel AI SDK

I've thoroughly explored the image generation implementation chain in the Vercel AI SDK. Here's the complete architecture from provider specifications through concrete implementations:

### 1. **Provider Specification Layer** (`@ai-sdk/provider`)

**Image Model Interfaces:**
- **ImageModelV3** (current) - `/packages/provider/src/image-model/v3/image-model-v3.ts`
  - `specificationVersion: 'v3'` - discriminator for version handling
  - `provider: string` - provider name for logging
  - `modelId: string` - provider-specific model ID
  - `maxImagesPerCall: number | undefined | GetMaxImagesPerCallFunction` - batch generation limits
  - `doGenerate(options: ImageModelV3CallOptions)` - core generation method
  
- **ImageModelV2** (legacy) - `/packages/provider/src/image-model/v2/image-model-v2.ts`
  - Similar structure but without some V3 features like file support and mask operations

**Core Call Options (V3)** - `/packages/provider/src/image-model/v3/image-model-v3-call-options.ts`:
- `prompt: string | undefined` - text prompt (optional for ops like upscaling)
- `n: number` - number of images
- `size: ${number}x${number} | undefined` - image dimensions (e.g., "1024x1024")
- `aspectRatio: ${number}:${number} | undefined` - aspect ratio format
- `seed: number | undefined` - reproducibility parameter
- `files: ImageModelV3File[] | undefined` - input images for editing/variations
- `mask: ImageModelV3File | undefined` - mask for inpainting
- `providerOptions: SharedV3ProviderOptions` - provider-specific settings
- `abortSignal?: AbortSignal` - cancellation support
- `headers?: Record<string, string | undefined>` - custom HTTP headers

**File Format** - `/packages/provider/src/image-model/v3/image-model-v3-file.ts`:
```typescript
ImageModelV3File = 
  | { type: 'file'; mediaType: string; data: string | Uint8Array; providerOptions?: ... }
  | { type: 'url'; url: string; providerOptions?: ... }
```

**Provider Interface** - `/packages/provider/src/provider/v3/provider-v3.ts`:
```typescript
interface ProviderV3 {
  imageModel(modelId: string): ImageModelV3;
  // ... other model types
}
```

### 2. **SDK Core Layer** (`@ai-sdk`)

**generateImage Function** - `/packages/ai/src/generate-image/generate-image.ts`:

Key features:
- Accepts `model: ImageModel` (can be V2 or V3)
- Prompt types: `string` or object with `{ images, text?, mask? }`
- Automatically batches requests based on `maxImagesPerCall` limits
- Parallelizes multiple API calls for generating N > maxImagesPerCall
- Normalizes prompts and converts various data formats
- Supports retry logic and abort signals
- Aggregates results across multiple calls

**Result Type** - `/packages/ai/src/generate-image/generate-image-result.ts`:
```typescript
GenerateImageResult {
  image: GeneratedFile;  // first image convenience accessor
  images: GeneratedFile[];
  warnings: Warning[];
  responses: ImageModelResponseMetadata[];
  providerMetadata: ImageModelProviderMetadata;
  usage: ImageModelUsage;
}
```

**Model Resolution** - `/packages/ai/src/model/as-image-model-v3.ts`:
- Converts V2 models to V3 via Proxy pattern for backward compatibility
- Logs compatibility warnings when V2 is used

### 3. **Provider Utilities Layer** (`@ai-sdk/provider-utils`)

**File Conversion Utility** - `/packages/provider-utils/src/convert-image-model-file-to-data-uri.ts`:
- Converts `ImageModelV3File` to data URI or URL strings
- Handles both base64 and Uint8Array data
- Preserves media types in data URIs

### 4. **Concrete Provider Implementation (OpenAI Example)**

**OpenAI Image Model Class** - `/packages/openai/src/image/openai-image-model.ts`:

Implements `ImageModelV3`:
- `maxImagesPerCall` getter returns model-specific limits (dall-e-3: 1, dall-e-2: 10, gpt-image-1: 10)
- `doGenerate()` handles two paths:
  1. **Image editing path** (when `files` provided):
     - Uses `/images/edits` endpoint
     - Converts `ImageModelV3File` objects to Blob format
     - Posts as form data with image and mask files
  2. **Standard generation path** (text-to-image):
     - Uses `/images/generations` endpoint
     - Posts JSON request
     - Enforces `b64_json` format for models without default response format

**Response Handling:**
- Parses response schema with Zod validation (openai-image-api.ts)
- Returns base64-encoded images or URLs
- Extracts and distributes token usage across generated images
- Provides provider metadata (revised prompts, creation time, quality settings)

**Provider Exposure** - `/packages/openai/src/openai-provider.ts`:
```typescript
createOpenAI() {
  const createImageModel = (modelId: OpenAIImageModelId) =>
    new OpenAIImageModel(modelId, {
      provider: `${providerName}.image`,
      url: ({ path }) => `${baseURL}${path}`,
      headers: getHeaders,
      fetch: options.fetch,
    });

  provider.image = createImageModel;
  provider.imageModel = createImageModel;  // dual naming pattern
}
```

### 5. **Middleware Support**

**Image Model Middleware** - `/packages/provider/src/image-model-middleware/v3/image-model-v3-middleware.ts`:

`ImageModelV3Middleware` interface enables:
- `transformParams()` - modify call parameters before API call
- `wrapGenerate()` - wrap the generation function for logging, caching, etc.
- `overrideProvider()`, `overrideModelId()`, `overrideMaxImagesPerCall()` - identity override

**Wrapper Function** - `/packages/ai/src/middleware/wrap-image-model.ts`:
- Composes multiple middlewares with proper parameter transformation pipeline
- Handles function binding for `maxImagesPerCall` implementations

### 6. **Data Flow Summary**

```
generateImage(model, prompt, options)
  ↓
resolveImageModel(modelArg) → ImageModelV3 or V2
  ↓
normalizePrompt(prompt) → { prompt, files, mask }
  ↓
calculateCallBatches(n, maxImagesPerCall)
  ↓
model.doGenerate(ImageModelV3CallOptions) [parallel calls]
  ↓
[Provider-specific] OpenAIImageModel.doGenerate()
  → convertToFormData/postJsonToApi()
  → API call (OpenAI /images/edits or /images/generations)
  ↓
Response aggregation & metadata collection
  ↓
GenerateImageResult { images, warnings, responses, providerMetadata, usage }
```

### Key Files Summary

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `/packages/provider/src/image-model/v3/image-model-v3.ts` | Core V3 specification |
| `/packages/provider/src/image-model/v3/image-model-v3-call-options.ts` | Call parameter types |
| `/packages/provider/src/image-model/v3/image-model-v3-file.ts` | File/URL union type |
| `/packages/ai/src/generate-image/generate-image.ts` | Main SDK function |
| `/packages/openai/src/image/openai-image-model.ts` | Concrete provider impl |
| `/packages/openai/src/openai-provider.ts` | Provider surface API |
| `/packages/provider-utils/src/convert-image-model-file-to-data-uri.ts` | Utility helper |
| `/packages/provider/src/image-model-middleware/v3/image-model-v3-middleware.ts` | Middleware protocol |

This architecture enables clean separation between the SDK abstraction layer, provider utilities, and concrete provider implementations while supporting backward compatibility with V2 models and flexible middleware composition.

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the image generation implementation. Let me create a detailed report.

## Summary Report: Image Generation in Vercel AI SDK

I've thoroughly explored the image generation implementation chain in the Vercel AI SDK. Here's the complete architecture from provider specifications through concrete implementations:

### 1. **Provider Specification Layer** (`@ai-sdk/provider`)

**Image Model Interfaces:**
- **ImageModelV3** (current) - `/packages/provider/src/image-model/v3/image-model-v3.ts`
  - `specificationVersion: 'v3'` - discriminator for version handling
  - `provider: string` - provider name for logging
  - `modelId: string` - provider-specific model ID
  - `maxImagesPerCall: number | undefined | GetMaxImagesPerCallFunction` - batch generation limits
  - `doGenerate(options: ImageModelV3CallOptions)` - core generation method
  
- **ImageModelV2** (legacy) - `/packages/provider/src/image-model/v2/image-model-v2.ts`
  - Similar structure but without some V3 features like file support and mask operations

**Core Call Options (V3)** - `/packages/provider/src/image-model/v3/image-model-v3-call-options.ts`:
- `prompt: string | undefined` - text prompt (optional for ops like upscaling)
- `n: number` - number of images
- `size: ${number}x${number} | undefined` - image dimensions (e.g., "1024x1024")
- `aspectRatio: ${number}:${number} | undefined` - aspect ratio format
- `seed: number | undefined` - reproducibility parameter
- `files: ImageModelV3File[] | undefined` - input images for editing/variations
- `mask: ImageModelV3File | undefined` - mask for inpainting
- `providerOptions: SharedV3ProviderOptions` - provider-specific settings
- `abortSignal?: AbortSignal` - cancellation support
- `headers?: Record<string, string | undefined>` - custom HTTP headers

**File Format** - `/packages/provider/src/image-model/v3/image-model-v3-file.ts`:
```typescript
ImageModelV3File = 
  | { type: 'file'; mediaType: string; data: string | Uint8Array; providerOptions?: ... }
  | { type: 'url'; url: string; providerOptions?: ... }
```

**Provider Interface** - `/packages/provider/src/provider/v3/provider-v3.ts`:
```typescript
interface ProviderV3 {
  imageModel(modelId: string): ImageModelV3;
  // ... other model types
}
```

### 2. **SDK Core Layer** (`@ai-sdk`)

**generateImage Function** - `/packages/ai/src/generate-image/generate-image.ts`:

Key features:
- Accepts `model: ImageModel` (can be V2 or V3)
- Prompt types: `string` or object with `{ images, text?, mask? }`
- Automatically batches requests based on `maxImagesPerCall` limits
- Parallelizes multiple API calls for generating N > maxImagesPerCall
- Normalizes prompts and converts various data formats
- Supports retry logic and abort signals
- Aggregates results across multiple calls

**Result Type** - `/packages/ai/src/generate-image/generate-image-result.ts`:
```typescript
GenerateImageResult {
  image: GeneratedFile;  // first image convenience accessor
  images: GeneratedFile[];
  warnings: Warning[];
  responses: ImageModelResponseMetadata[];
  providerMetadata: ImageModelProviderMetadata;
  usage: ImageModelUsage;
}
```

**Model Resolution** - `/packages/ai/src/model/as-image-model-v3.ts`:
- Converts V2 models to V3 via Proxy pattern for backward compatibility
- Logs compatibility warnings when V2 is used

### 3. **Provider Utilities Layer** (`@ai-sdk/provider-utils`)

**File Conversion Utility** - `/packages/provider-utils/src/convert-image-model-file-to-data-uri.ts`:
- Converts `ImageModelV3File` to data URI or URL strings
- Handles both base64 and Uint8Array data
- Preserves media types in data URIs

### 4. **Concrete Provider Implementation (OpenAI Example)**

**OpenAI Image Model Class** - `/packages/openai/src/image/openai-image-model.ts`:

Implements `ImageModelV3`:
- `maxImagesPerCall` getter returns model-specific limits (dall-e-3: 1, dall-e-2: 10, gpt-image-1: 10)
- `doGenerate()` handles two paths:
  1. **Image editing path** (when `files` provided):
     - Uses `/images/edits` endpoint
     - Converts `ImageModelV3File` objects to Blob format
     - Posts as form data with image and mask files
  2. **Standard generation path** (text-to-image):
     - Uses `/images/generations` endpoint
     - Posts JSON request
     - Enforces `b64_json` format for models without default response format

**Response Handling:**
- Parses response schema with Zod validation (openai-image-api.ts)
- Returns base64-encoded images or URLs
- Extracts and distributes token usage across generated images
- Provides provider metadata (revised prompts, creation time, quality settings)

**Provider Exposure** - `/packages/openai/src/openai-provider.ts`:
```typescript
createOpenAI() {
  const createImageModel = (modelId: OpenAIImageModelId) =>
    new OpenAIImageModel(modelId, {
      provider: `${providerName}.image`,
      url: ({ path }) => `${baseURL}${path}`,
      headers: getHeaders,
      fetch: options.fetch,
    });

  provider.image = createImageModel;
  provider.imageModel = createImageModel;  // dual naming pattern
}
```

### 5. **Middleware Support**

**Image Model Middleware** - `/packages/provider/src/image-model-middleware/v3/image-model-v3-middleware.ts`:

`ImageModelV3Middleware` interface enables:
- `transformParams()` - modify call parameters before API call
- `wrapGenerate()` - wrap the generation function for logging, caching, etc.
- `overrideProvider()`, `overrideModelId()`, `overrideMaxImagesPerCall()` - identity override

**Wrapper Function** - `/packages/ai/src/middleware/wrap-image-model.ts`:
- Composes multiple middlewares with proper parameter transformation pipeline
- Handles function binding for `maxImagesPerCall` implementations

### 6. **Data Flow Summary**

```
generateImage(model, prompt, options)
  ↓
resolveImageModel(modelArg) → ImageModelV3 or V2
  ↓
normalizePrompt(prompt) → { prompt, files, mask }
  ↓
calculateCallBatches(n, maxImagesPerCall)
  ↓
model.doGenerate(ImageModelV3CallOptions) [parallel calls]
  ↓
[Provider-specific] OpenAIImageModel.doGenerate()
  → convertToFormData/postJsonToApi()
  → API call (OpenAI /images/edits or /images/generations)
  ↓
Response aggregation & metadata collection
  ↓
GenerateImageResult { images, warnings, responses, providerMetadata, usage }
```

### Key Files Summary

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `/packages/provider/src/image-model/v3/image-model-v3.ts` | Core V3 specification |
| `/packages/provider/src/image-model/v3/image-model-v3-call-options.ts` | Call parameter types |
| `/packages/provider/src/image-model/v3/image-model-v3-file.ts` | File/URL union type |
| `/packages/ai/src/generate-image/generate-image.ts` | Main SDK function |
| `/packages/openai/src/image/openai-image-model.ts` | Concrete provider impl |
| `/packages/openai/src/openai-provider.ts` | Provider surface API |
| `/packages/provider-utils/src/convert-image-model-file-to-data-uri.ts` | Utility helper |
| `/packages/provider/src/image-model-middleware/v3/image-model-v3-middleware.ts` | Middleware protocol |

This architecture enables clean separation between the SDK abstraction layer, provider utilities, and concrete provider implementations while supporting backward compatibility with V2 models and flexible middleware composition.

## Summary

After thorough exploration of the codebase (using `packages/fal` as the primary template — it's the closest analog: a clean image-focused provider following all current SDK patterns), here's what the `@ai-sdk/pixelart` package requires:

---

### Files to Create (15 new files)

| File | Description |
|---|---|
| `packages/pixelart/package.json` | Package metadata, version `0.0.0`, same shape as fal |
| `packages/pixelart/tsconfig.json` | Extends `@vercel/ai-tsconfig/ts-library.json`, refs `provider` + `provider-utils` |
| `packages/pixelart/tsconfig.build.json` | Same as fal — disables `composite` for tsup |
| `packages/pixelart/tsup.config.ts` | Builds CJS + ESM, injects version, same as fal |
| `packages/pixelart/vitest.node.config.js` | `environment: 'node'` |
| `packages/pixelart/vitest.edge.config.js` | `environment: 'edge-runtime'` |
| `packages/pixelart/turbo.json` | `extends: ["//"]`, outputs `dist/**` |
| `src/version.ts` | `__PACKAGE_VERSION__` injection pattern |
| `src/pixelart-image-settings.ts` | `PixelArtImageModelId` union: `'pixelart/standard' \| 'pixelart/retro' \| 'pixelart/8bit' \| (string & {})` |
| `src/pixelart-image-options.ts` | Zod schema with `style: enum(['pixel','retro','8bit'])` and `pixelDensity: number (1–100)` |
| `src/pixelart-image-model.ts` | `PixelArtImageModel implements ImageModelV3` — POSTs to `/images/generate`, parses `{ data: [{b64_json}] }` response |
| `src/pixelart-provider.ts` | `createPixelArt()` factory, `PixelArtProvider extends ProviderV3`, throws `NoSuchModelError` for language/embedding |
| `src/index.ts` | Re-exports everything |
| `src/pixelart-image-model.test.ts` | Tests request building, provider options mapping, base64→Uint8Array decode, error handling |
| `src/pixelart-provider.test.ts` | Tests factory shape, `NoSuchModelError` for unsupported model types |

### File to Modify (1 existing file)

- **`/tsconfig.json`** (root) — add `{ "path": "packages/pixelart" }` in alphabetical order

---

### Key Design Decisions

- **Auth**: `Authorization: Bearer <key>` via `PIXELART_API_KEY` env var, using the `loadApiKey` utility from `@ai-sdk/provider-utils`
- **API**: `POST https://api.pixelart.ai/v1/images/generate` with `{ model, prompt, n, size, seed, style?, pixel_density? }`
- **Response**: `{ created, data: [{ b64_json }] }` — base64 decoded directly to `Uint8Array` (no URL-download step)
- **Provider options** (`style`, `pixelDensity`) accessed via `providerOptions.pixelart` using `parseProviderOptions`
- **Unsupported models**: `languageModel()` and `embeddingModel()` throw `NoSuchModelError`
